content.problemStatement: You are given an integer NNN. Find the largest integer between 111 and 101010 (inclusive) which divides NNN.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the largest divisor of N that lies in the inclusive range [1, 10].

sampleTestCases.explanation: Divisors of 91 are 1,7,13,91. Among numbers 1..10 the divisors are 1 and 7; the largest is 7.
Divisors of 24 are 1,2,3,4,6,8,12,24. Within 1..10 the maximum divisor is 8.
13 is prime; its only divisor in the range 1..10 is 1.

solution.approach: For each N we only need to check the ten possible candidates 10,9,…,1. Starting from 10 and moving downwards, the first value that divides N is the answer because we are scanning in decreasing order. This runs in O(10) per test case, i.e., O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work (at most 10 checks) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
